message:
docs(wasm): add CPU RUSTFLAGS suppression task to Phase 1 TODO

Add task to document approach for suppressing CPU-specific RUSTFLAGS noise during
wasm builds. This addresses the secondary blocker noted in PHASE1_FINDINGS.md:
warning about -C target-cpu=znver3 being ignored on wasm32 targets.

Proposed solution (from PHASE1_FINDINGS.md): Use target-specific RUSTFLAGS in
.cargo/config.toml to apply CPU flags only to native x86_64 targets, leaving
wasm builds clean.

Related to Phase 2.1 scaffolding task: Configure .cargo/config.toml before
rerunning cargo check.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -42,6 +42,7 @@ _Created: 2025-10-22 (UTC). Keep this list exhaustive; mark completion inline wi
   - [x] Enable `getrandom` WebAssembly backend via workspace feature flags (Cargo.toml updated).
   - [ ] Gate Rayon behind feature flag or `cfg` for wasm, documenting fallback path.
   - [x] Re-run `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` and append findings.
+  - [ ] Document approach for suppressing CPU-specific `RUSTFLAGS` noise during wasm builds.
 
 - [x] Security baseline
   - [x] Draft `docs/wasm/SECURITY_NOTES_PHASE1.md` covering COOP/COEP, CSP, SAB requirements.
